desc:
The road looks just as unattended here as everywhere else. Grass and ferns cover
most of it, however the seemingly once so majestic way can still be discerned
where the vegetation is sparse. The trees seem to have been closing in on the
road over the years, making it a lot narrower than in its glory days.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road has a gravel surface here, with green grass and wild bushes growing in
profusion over it. It makes a long bend from south to the west, following the
edges of a high cliff that can be observed in the east, high above the road.
Dark-green bushes, with long, sharp thorns grow on the north side of the road.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
The Old Forest Road turns significantly here and continues to the east and
north. The road has not seen any maintenance for centuries, and at places it is
just a wide trail between the bushes, not a road anymore. Tall trees loom above,
their dense foliage casting deep shadows upon the road.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
What once was a well-kept, well-travelled passage through the forest of
Mirkwood, has decayed over the centuries into a faint memory of its former self,
being now an overgrown, grassy path that runs through the menacing forest.
Bordered by a steep cliff and dense, black willows, the road continues to the
west and east.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
A lush carpet of grass gives barely any hint that the Old Forest Road here was
once a majestic, busy passageway through Mirkwood Forest. Occasional old tracks
are barely visible through the grass and forest debris of littered leaves,
fallen branches, and loose rocks.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
An old path, at times only two or three yards wide, is all that remains of the
Old Forest Road as it runs through dense shrubbery of black wild bushes. From
above the road is shadowed by thick, leaf-covered branches and hanging vines
which totally block the sky from view. Tough, sharp-looking weeds grow on the
ground and beyond the brush stand the towering, huge trees of Mirkwood.
name: Overgrown Forest Road

desc:
The Old Forest Road makes a long, sweeping bend here, following the upwards
slope of a grass-covered ridge towards the north. To the west it leads down the
ridge and into the dark shadows of Mirkwood forest. The road itself is mostly
overgrown with grass and weed, although a trampled path in the middle of the
ancient road is proof that it is still used occasionally.
name: A Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
Although the wilderness has taken back most of the Old Forest Road, with grass
and shrubs now growing on the hard-packed earth, it would take many years still
for the last traces of the road to vanish. Large, brown bushes dot the ridge,
sprawling thorny things often several yards across.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The ancient road moves along the highest point of the ridge, where the
grass-covered ground is flat and solid. There are few obstacles to force the
road to bend or detour up here and it follows a straight route from east to
west. The grass grows high and wild, swaying gently whenever a breeze stirs the
air.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The dense grass grows several feet tall, long stalks swaying gently whenever a
breeze stirs the air. Many different types of bushes and shrubs also thrive on
the gently rolling ridge, most of them tangled masses full of prickly thorns.
The Old Forest Road follows the highest point of the ridge from east to west.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
This wide hollow is filled with a dense growth of large, tangled bushes which
form a complex mess of vegetation all around the area. Towards the east and
west, the ground seems to rise softly in the form of two long, grass-covered
slopes. Little can be seen of the Old Forest Road, the thoroughfare being mainly
discernible by a lessened density in undergrowth.
name: In a Brush-filled Hollow

desc:
The grass-covered slope rises in a soft curve from west to east and the Old
Forest Road follows the slope faithfully towards the top of the ridge. The grass
grows high and wild and is interrupted only by clusters of large, thorny bushes.
Dark trees of Mirkwood lean over the road on both sides, their branches almost
meeting above.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Giant, oppressive trees stand tall and silent, their canopy enveloping the
forest below. On the ground level dense thickets and numerous spider webs make
it hard to traverse this area. Mosses and ferns have spread throughout the
undergrowth. Some fallen trees lie here, slowly decomposing and acting as a
valuable source of nutrients for the green and white mould that resides on them.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
These hills bear very little life, only a sprinkling of grass and some fairly
gnarled bushes. Eastward, the ominous border of Mirkwood can be observed, dark
and thick. Erupting from the ground here and there are rocks of various sizes
and shapes.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The hills here begin to support more life as evidenced by the thickening stands
of oak and birch. A light ground-covering of sparse grass provides a soft layer
of yellow-green while small, dark green bushes sprout up here and there. Among
the rocks a rare sight is visible: a trickle of clear water emerging from the
hillside, running among the rocks, and disappearing again to the ground. Birds
not brave enough to enter Mirkwood directly, make their homes here in relative
safety.
name: Grass-covered Hills

desc:
The bald steep hills stretch in every direction. The ground is barren, desolate
and bare of any living vegetation. Loose rocks and smaller sharp chips of rock
cover the ground, making walking on foot difficult and unpleasant. There is an
abrupt downhill to the west, and almost vertical wall of rock to the east. So
steep are the hills that one cannot see a single tree, despite being actually
surrounded by the giant forest.
name: Hills in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
Great, serpentine vines cling to the trunks of various trees, climbing upwards
until they disappear between the boughs above. The forest has no distinguishing
features by which to orientate, it is all ancient, twisted trees casting black
shadows in the perpetual gloom. Tangles of thorny brush grow throughout the
forest, with occasional cobwebs being spun between the trees.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Dying and rotting trees litter the landscape as Mirkwood turns a sickly grey
pallor. It seems as if a sickness or power has been spreading from the south for
ages now, causing decay and destruction. Even the fungi and lichens cannot seem
to survive here in this sickly woods.
name: Dying Forest

desc:
The overhanging tree branches and hanging cobwebs create a sad, dismal feel to
Mirkwood. It seems that after being angry for so long, the trees have fallen
over, leaning against each other in gloomy resignation. Even the thorny bushes
and lesser vegetation here are thin and puny. Everything seems bowed under the
weight of ages and permanent darkness.
name: Dismal Forest

desc:
Massive tangles of evergreen, coniferous shrub grow beneath the dark canopy of
the Mirkwood. The large, thorny bushes are interlaced with climbing vines and
strands of grey lichen. The gloomy forest consist chiefly of oak and beech,
although there are rowan, maple and yew present.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Stands of fir and beech are interspersed with the gnarled, ancient trunks of
oak. The ground is littered by dead leaves, fir needles and cones. Large,
evergreen juniper bushes and smaller, bristly shrubs also flourish here. Towards
the north there is a grassy slope rising from the forest's shadow but the way to
the top of it is blocked by the dense undergrowth. Instead, the dark mouth of a
tunnel looms ahead.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
The shadows are deep and many here in the depths of Mirkwood where the ground
has not been touched by the bright rays of the sun for countless years. The soft
soil is covered with brown leaves and fir needles, and various types of
mushrooms and fungi grow in glum shadows. Towards the east and south, large
bushes covered in sharp thorns block the way.
name: Shadowy Woodlands

desc:
Soft, rustling sounds can be heard from above, where the wind constantly shakes
and stirs the crowns of the trees. Here beneath the dense cover of branches and
leaves, the shadows are deep and many, and the light of the sun may not have
touched the ground for hundreds of years. Most of the trees are gnarled and
hoary, overgrown with fungi and vines. The undergrowth is sparse, but there are
dense tangles of large, thorny shrubs towards the north and south.
name: Shadowy Woodlands

desc:
This narrow, cramped tunnel is filled with a musky, earthy smell. The soil is
soft and moist and smells of rotting leaves and worms. Small clumps of earth
fall from the tunnel's roof occasionally and should it collapse the entire
tunnel and everything in it would be buried.
name: A Cramped Earth Tunnel

desc:
This damp earthen tunnel leads out into Mirkwood towards the north, where the
forest lies as dark as the inside of this passageway, and continues onwards
toward the south. It is beyond a doubt created by someone or something and not a
natural thing, for it runs straight as an arrow through the soft soil.
name: A Cramped Earth Tunnel

desc:
The ground is littered with a multitude of dead leaves mixed with fir cones and
needles. Thorny thickets grow across the area, encroaching on the dark, gnarled
boles and open spaces in between trees alike. Above, the heavy boughs and
intertwining branches of the tree tops form a roof that separates the forest
floor from the outside world.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Deep shadows and impenetrable darkness stalk between the gnarled trunks of
ancient oaks. Little light ever finds its way through the crowns of the trees
down to the forest floor. Yet even in the eternal gloom there is life in the
form of large thickets of sprawling, thorny bushes that creak and rustle softly
as the dense, intertwined branches brush against one another.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest here has no distinguishing features. The dark shadows and dense woods
conceal any useful landmarks. There is nothing to be seen in any direction but
never ending rows of dark, gnarled trunks rising up to meet a dense canopy
above. One thick bole looks much like the next, hoary and gnarled, overgrown
with lichen and clumps of fungi. The ground is soft and damp and covered by dead
leaves that rustle softly whenever stepped on.
name: Deep in a Gloomy Forest

desc:
The dense forest lies shrouded in shadows and gloom. The tree tops above are so
thick with intertwining branches and foliage that the sky beyond can barely be
glimpsed. The ground around the boles is covered with knotted roots, clumps of
small, brown mushrooms and dead leaves.
name: Shaded Woodlands

desc:
Large, thorny bushes grow between the dark trunks of oak and beech, at places
completely filling the space between the tree-trunks with masses of tangled,
intertwined vegetation. The southern part of the area has the highest density of
undergrowth and looks all but impassable. To the north passage is blocked by
jagged rocks rising from the forest floor to form a formidable obstacle, the
highest tips disappearing into the thick canopy above.
name: South of a Rocky Outcropping

desc:
A large variety of bushes grow here, tangled in large masses of impassable
vegetation, many of them with sharp thorns ready to snag and prick the unwary.
There is a constant rustling from the deeper parts of the undergrowth, seemingly
from beneath piles of decaying leaves that fill the air with a faint smell of
rot, yet the small forest creatures causing the noises can but rarely be
glimpsed.
name: In an Overgrown Corner of Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A massive oak has been uprooted here and now lies on the ground rotting away. A
large, uneven hole in the ground shows where the large roots now reaching up
into the air were previously buried. Several smaller trees lie crushed beneath
the fallen behemoth. The decaying trunk has become the nesting ground to swarms
of small, black insects that fill the area with a faint droning buzz. A
corpse-grey lichen can be seen on many of the trees, growing in strange patterns
on the dark trunks.
name: At a Fallen Oak

desc:
A colony of white aspen grows amidst the beech and oak of the surrounding
forest, their thinner, pale trunks a stark contrast to the otherwise dark and
gnarled trees. The canopy above is no less dense and impregnable than elsewhere
in the forest and there is not the slightest hint of a sky above. The ground
rises towards the south but passage in that direction is blocked by a tangled
mass of large, thorn-covered brush.
name: Forest at the Foot of a Slope

desc:
The Old Forest Road follows an unswerving path along the top of the ridge on its
course from east to west. Only at one point is the ancient pathway forced to a
small detour by a rocky outcropping. Nature is working hard at reclaiming the
old road, grass and weed sprouting up from the hard, solid earth that has seen
centuries of use by wagon wheels and tramping feet.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Most of the ancient road that leads along the top of this ridge from east to
west is now overgrown by grass, dense brush and low trees. Yet there is a well
trampled pathway in the middle of the old road that seems to be in use still.
The dark canopy of Mirkwood rises to meet the descending slope to the south and
north, with large, tangled bushes in the shadow of the trees forming an all but
impassable wall of thorns and intertwining branches.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The old road follows the flat, high ground at the top of a grass-covered ridge
on its way through Mirkwood forest. Next to the road there are several low walls
of stone visible in the high grass. The ruins, perhaps once a way station or inn
along the road, are long since overgrown with moss and lichen. There are signs
of recent battle among the ruins. Broken pieces of armour and weapons lie
scattered among the grass, and there are several dark splotches that could be
blood.
name: Old Forest Road by a Ruin

desc:
The Old Forest Road, although largely overgrown with grass and weed, is still
visible against the denser vegetation to the sides where it follows the high
point of the ridge towards the east and west. Large bushes sprawl across the
downwards slopes to the north and south.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The grass-covered ridge begins a slow, gentle descent towards the east. The Old
Forest Road cuts a straight, unswerving path through undergrowth and high grass.
A few boulders, overgrown with moss and lichen, dot the landscape like dark,
hulking shapes half hidden by the brush.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The Old Forest Road runs on top of a small ridge. The road makes a lazy curve
following the natural shapes of the terrain, ascending to the west and
descending slowly to the south, avoiding the forested slopes to the east and
north. The gentle slopes give no trouble for travellers, although the
surrounding forest constantly presses in around the road, as though threatening
to swallow the road completely along with any unwary traveller.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
Tall, dark trees surrounding the road make it impossible to see the sky above.
To the north the road starts to climb along a gentle slope of a ridge and the
forest opens up slightly in that direction. Eastwards the road continues on its
east-west course through the thick and inhospitable forest.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The perpetual gloom here, caused by the green canopy above, ends towards the
north where a steep slope littered with large rocks leads upwards into the
hills. Large, sprawling bushes overgrown with black and grey lichen can be seen
in all directions and they seem to flourish here in the dark, protected from the
glare of the sun.
name: Beneath a Dark Canopy

desc:
Great, web-covered trunks rise up from the ground, their upper parts hidden from
view by a dense growth of branches and leaves. Silky spider webs extend from
trunk to trunk and from bush to bush, blocking passageways and steering
travellers deeper into Mirkwood. The brooding silence and eternal gloom of the
forest is interrupted to the south where a slope can be glimpsed beyond an
impassable tangle of thorny bushes.
name: Spider Web-covered Thicket

desc:
Neither paths nor trails exist in this tangled part of the forest, and passage
is limited to travelling upon patches of black moss between the towering tree
trunks and large, sprawling bushes. A continuous, foreboding web has been spun
above, entangling branches, vines and black leaves. Several dead trees lie
rotting on the ground, the decomposing trunks covered by lichen and large
clusters of brown fungi.
name: Under the Spider Webs

desc:
The ground slopes slightly downwards from the east and south towards this dark,
wet corner of the Mirkwood. Motley patches of moss and lichen are intermingled
with shallow pools filled with rotting vegetation and dead leaves. Animal life
seems to thrive amidst the decomposition and there is a constant humming,
buzzing and droning from the multitudes of insects that make up the menu for
various frogs and lizards.
name: Dark, Decaying Grove

desc:
Strands of grey, foul-smelling lichen cover the bushes and shrubs growing here,
giving the impression of large, tangled spiders' webs haphazardly strewn across
the forest. The darkness beneath the intertwining branches above is complete and
there is not a hint of sky to be glimpsed. Passage towards the east is blocked
by jagged rocky outcroppings.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Small bushes with tiny dark leaves and many needle-sharp thorns grow in clusters
around the dark trunks of ancient beech trees. Where old trees have fallen and
now lie rotting on the ground, the bushes grow over and on top of the fallen
trunks, sprouting from the rotten bark like parasitic fungi. There is a faint
smell of corruption and rot in the air.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Large, thorn-covered bushes grow along the edge of a rocky outcropping to the
south. The rocks protrude from the earth like sets of uneven, jagged teeth, five
or six yards high. The dark canopy above stretches out above the rocks, long
branches extending across the gap to meet other branches, likewise reaching out
from the other side.
name: North of a Rocky Outcropping

desc:
Most of the forest in this part of Mirkwood consists of tall, stout oaks, all of
them ancient, hoary and overgrown with fungi. Tangles of thick, twisted vines
snake up along the gnarled trunks, twisting around boles and branches as if
trying to choke the life out of them.
name: Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
Dense thickets grow in the shadows of the ancient trees of Mirkwood. Towards the
south, part of a grassy slope is visible beyond a wall of thorny bushes. The
ground is covered with dead, decaying leaves that fill the air with the smell of
decomposing plants.
name: Shadowy Thickets

desc:
The ancient trees loom in the dark gloom like gnarled, twisted shades, deeper
black shapes in the encroaching shadows. There is a constant soft, rustling
sound from the tree crowns above, where perhaps winds sweep across the roof of
the forest. The ground is covered by dead leaves in various stages of decay.
name: Dark Corner of Mirkwood

desc:
Old gnarled trunks of oak rise up from the forest floor to disappear in the
canopy above. The trunks are covered in patches of lichen and fungi, and
entangled in spider webs. At places on the great trunks, there are deep marks
and scratches as if by claws. From these wounds dark, yellow sap has poured
forth and dried on the bark.
name: Among Dark, Looming Oaks

desc:
The deep shadows of Mirkwood extend to the very base of a grass-covered slope to
the south, where a dense tangle of thorn-covered bushes takes over where the
trees end. Various shrubs and bushes seem to thrive here in the dark even though
they are never touched by the bright rays of the sun that are usually essential
for plant life.
name: Dark Woods at the Foot of a Slope

desc:
A grassy slope can be glimpsed to the north, through and beyond an impassable
wall of brambles and dense, thorny vegetation. Patches of black moss grow on the
ground in between thick, knotted roots and several clusters of grey mushrooms
sprout up in dense clumps among the moss. A faint smell of decay seems to
emanate from the moss or the mushrooms, or possibly both.
name: Dark Woods south of a Ridge

desc:
Several of the massive, ancient oaks growing here seem affected by some kind of
disease. The bark has fallen off from large areas of the gnarled trunks. The
whole area around the trees is covered by a thick, foul-smelling cobweb. Passage
to the north and south is blocked by a tangled mass of large, thorny bushes.
name: Sticky Foul-smelling Web

desc:
Lush, evergreen foliage encroaches upon any open space here on the forest floor
beneath the dense boughs of Mirkwood. The boughs above are thick and the canopy
dense, and even during the brightest of days, only slivers of light would reach
the ground. Beyond the impassable tangles of thorny brush to the north, there is
a grassy slope leading upwards to a ridge.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The earth is covered by layers of dark green fir needles. The forest is shrouded
in deep shadows and little light ever finds the way through the thick foliage
above down to the forest floor. The woods have an air of ages immemorial about
them and many of these trees have probably stood here for centuries.
name: Dusky Forest

desc:
This entire area of Mirkwood is cast in eternal gloom, with a dense canopy above
shutting out the outside world. The twisted shapes of ancient trees loom like
obscure, menacing shadows in the encroaching darkness. The forest floor is
covered by layers of decaying leaves and green fir needles.
name: Deep in a Dismal Forest

desc:
Dark, malignant forest spreads in all directions. The numerous roots and dense,
black bushes that grow all around the area make travelling difficult. A group of
twisted trees looks strangely different than the other surrounding trees; with
their hunchback trunks, dried branch arms and lichen beards they closely
resemble a band of old, withered men, eternally frozen to the positions where
they stand.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
A small clearing opens up under the black, web-infested canopy of Mirkwood. The
opening is surrounded by thorn-bushes and a tangled network of vines that hang
from the boughs above. Grey spider webs of different sizes have been woven
throughout the area; one especially vast web is located to the south, completely
blocking the route towards that direction. A road is barely visible behind the
trees to the north.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Mirkwood gets very dense here, as if being reluctant to allow one to travel any
further, and trying to hinder and resist every single step forward. Giant, dark
bushes block the openings between the trees and gnarly roots reach forward,
tripping and trapping the travellers. A malignant, grim aura lies above the
forest - the forest is listening, watching and waiting.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
A multitude of dead leaves in hues of brown and green are ensnared among the
dense shrubbery here. The ground is covered in yet more dead leaves that lie
piled up against trunks of trees and beneath the shrubs and bushes. The area is
dark and visibility very limited due to the deep, dark shadows and heavy
foliage.
name: Deep in the Tangle

desc:
At the bottom of the ridge, the dark forest grows quickly dense and entangled, a
mass of dark oaks with tightly-entwined branches forming a solid roof above the
ground, shutting out all light. Loose gravel fills the side of the ridge facing
this way, making it impossible to ascend to the west from here. To the south a
mass of dense, high bushes grow making it impossible to pass that way.
name: Forest below a Ridge

desc:
A huge and tall oak dominates the area, reaching high above the other trees,
though how high is impossible to tell due to the thick and entwined crowns of
the other trees. The forest seems open enough to the north and west, while to
the east and south the trees grow far too close to each other to allow passage
through, or even seeing through.
name: A Tall Oak in the Dark Woods

desc:
The branches of this old oak are strong and broad, forming a natural landing in
the tree. Using the boughs, one can proceed further into the canopy without
touching the ground of the forest at all. There are old cobwebs and
freshly-woven, oozing spider webs on the boughs, indicating that spiders are
living in these trees.
name: Inside the Lower Canopy

desc:
A delicate, shimmering spider web is hanging in the air next to the blackened
trunk of an ancient, giant elm tree. Being finely-woven, the strands of the web
seem strong and robust even when the silky threads themselves are quite thin.
The strong branches of the elm form bridges to nearby trees, while the swaying
spider web can be used as a ladder to proceed upwards, into the canopy.
name: Grey, Silky Ladders

desc:
A spiralling, dark tunnel rises steadily in the trees. It has taken its course
from the natural shapes of the forest, following supporting boughs and circling
around the trunks which, together with grey, sticky spider webs, form the walls
of this tunnel. The visibility in this long, twisting passageway is very
limited, reaching to the next turn of the tunnel, but never further.
name: Cobwebbed Tube

desc:
There is a myriad of webbed tunnels in this area, forming a maze-like network as
they twist to and fro, with passages leading in practically every direction.
Sometimes the tunnels cross each other, sometimes they stop abruptly at dead
ends where sticky spider webs block further travel.
name: Dark, Twisting, Cobwebbed Tunnels

desc:
This sticky, webbed platform offers a view under the dark canopy. Ancient trees
stand like giants in the darkness, their crowns joining together somewhere
above, beyond the range of visibility. Platforms of some kind have been
constructed on the nearby trees, here and there connected to each other by rope
bridges made of thick spider webs. An exceptionally large net-like web hangs
close to this platform, descending downwards like a giant curtain.
name: Hanging Webs

desc:
Masses of dark lichens are hanging from the gnarled and bent branches protruding
from the wide, dark-grey and black, hulking boles of the twisted trees. They
form gloomy curtains in the forest that have to be passed through to travel
forward. The visibility is poor, but what one can see indicates that the
vine-covered trees are growing too densely in the west and south to allow
passage.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Underneath the thick, continuous canopy that covers the forest and envelops it
in perpetual darkness, narrow passageways twist between the hulking, dark boles
of the trees. To the west the low-hanging branches are covered with some sort of
dark vines, and a large mass of thin but heavily-entwined thorny shrub grows
densely in the east.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The thick forest has created a corridor leading to the north and south; the
moss-covered trees lean in on each other, forming a fairly easily travelled,
though very narrow, passageway in the seemingly ever-present darkness. Little
besides the dark trees and some equally dark vines grow here.
name: Corridor in the Dark Woods

desc:
Large piles of crushed, uneven boulders litter the northern part of the forest,
at the foot of a near-vertical rock face. The top of the rock face is hidden
from view by the dense vegetation of the treetops. In between the gnarled trunks
of beech and oak, the ground is covered by decomposing layers of old leaves and
patches of black moss.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
These bare hills rise above the darkness of Mirkwood below. Few plants thrive
here, there is no undergrowth except small, brown shrubs that grow wherever
their roots have found cracks and irregularities in the rocks. A deep crack
separates these hills from a stony plateau to the east, deepening into a ravine
that continues out of sight below.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Small rocks litter this ledge, but a part of the area has been cleared of stones
and a primitive shelter of wood and brush has been built as protection from the
wind. A deep ravine to the east would make any fall from this ledge fatal, and
with the steep, broken slopes blocking passage to the north and west, the only
reasonable way off this ledge lies to the south.
name: Outcropping above a Ravine

desc:
The trees of this area are tall, dark and twisted. The boughs hang low, full of
black leaves and sticky-looking spider webs. Some smaller animals have got
themselves entangled in the webs, and ended up being hopelessly stuck. The
forest is open enough for travelling to the north and east, while a forested
ridge stretches out to the southwest.
name: Forest below a Ridge

desc:
Dark trees twist and claw their way upwards towards the sky, competing for space
and light, and leaving none for the inhabitants of the lower layers of the
forest. A particularly twisted and dense patch of trees makes up an impassable
obstacle northwards; and while travel in all other directions is possible, easy
it is not.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Tangled beech trees grow all around this small opening, making travel hard but
not impossible, except to the north where a thick thorn hedge intermingles with
the beech trees, creating a dense, impassable barrier. Exposed roots snake
across the ground, at times camouflaged by piles of leaves and twigs, making the
footing precarious.
name: Tangled Beeches

desc:
The forest forms an impenetrable ceiling out of branches, leaves and vines,
blanketing the woods in perpetual darkness. The ground is a shady mixture of
shrubs, thick masses of vines and gnarled trees. One can proceed in all
directions save the north, where the rugged terrain rises too steeply.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
A vast expanse of thick, black moss thrives in this part of the forest. Few
bushes and even fewer trees grow here, and the black moss covers the ground
completely like a large, dark blanket. The black moss crumbles in on itself at
the slightest touch, filling the air with the heavy scents of rot and decay.
name: On an Expanse of Black Moss

desc:
The Forest of Mirkwood emanates a peculiar feeling of warning here. Intruders
are clearly not welcome beyond this point; growing plants seem to catch or snag
at clothing in proof of this feeling, the trees seem to follow any unusual
movement, and dangling spider webs and mosses tremble slightly even though there
is no wind of any kind.
name: Forbidding Forest

desc:
The hills here are lush with green grass and scattered low bushes. The ground is
soft, albeit difficult to walk on with its numerous ups and downs, especially to
the north the terrain ascends rapidly. In the distance, the great forest can be
seen, stretching towards the horizon.
name: Grassy Mound

desc:
The grass-covered hills roll and bounce around in all directions, gradually
descending in height to the south. Dense shrub thickets and tall clumps of grass
and weeds dot the landscape, intermixed with occasional patches of rock and
boulders.
name: Grassy Hills

desc:
The ground here is covered with stones and is difficult to walk on. The flora on
these hills is limited to some small patches of grass, struggling against the
harsh surroundings. The view to the west is blocked by a steeply ascending
hillside. There seem to be small cracks high up on the rock wall, and the rock
beneath these are covered in white streaks.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The hills here are almost completely sterile and little to no vegetation can be
found, except for some patches of grass, fighting for survival. Sharp rocks lie
scattered over the hill, making travelling by foot difficult. To the west, a
rock face can be seen, stretching southwards.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
These rocky, windswept hills are almost devoid of wildlife. Tufts of tough
mountain grass grow in cracks in the stony ground and a crust like grey lichen
can be seen on the southern side of many of the large boulders that litter the
area.
name: Stony Hills

desc:
These harsh, stony hills nourish few plants and only the most enduring of trees,
yet even the trees that do grow here are twisted and stunted things. The hills
slope downwards to the east and the trees grow closer together, and taller, in
that direction.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
A variety of trees and undergrowth thrive on these hills. Rocky outcroppings and
large boulders covered in grey lichen are interspersed throughout the forest.
The rockier terrain is mainly found towards the west and south where the ground
rises higher into the hills.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
Great, dark trunks of ancient trees, overgrown with moss and lichen, rise up
from the forest floor towards the unseen sky above. The branches stretching out
in all directions, thick and heavily-leaved, blot out the sky and cause these
parts of the Mirkwood to lie in a perpetual gloom.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Several enormous dark oaks dominate this part of the Mirkwood forest. Their
trunks are at least ten feet thick and are covered wholly by a pitch-black
lichen that emanates a smell of rot and decay so thick that it could almost be
touched. Piles of rotting black leaves cover the ground, adding their smell to
that of the lichen.
name: Deep in Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Little undergrowth can be seen on the leaf-covered ground between the tall
trunks of the old, gnarled Mirkwood trees. A silent row of giant cocoons wrapped
in spider web is hanging from the black branches, some of them occasionally
swinging in the air. A thick canopy above blocks out the sky and sun. Although
visibility is short, limited by darkness and the trees, the forest seems to
stretch out endlessly in all directions with no hints of a change in the
terrain.
name: Cocoons Hanging in the Dark

desc:
Layers of leaves in various stages of decay cover the forest floor. Thick,
knotted roots poke through the rotting mass here and there, and at other places
bumps and curves beneath the fallen leaves hint at more roots. The forest is
encompassed in a deep, brooding silence and there is no hint of a change in the
terrain in any direction.
name: Deep in Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The shadows lie deep beneath the roof of the forest. Not even the slightest hint
of sky can be seen through the densely intertwined branches above. Towards the
west the ground rises steeply and the forest seems to come to a halt at the foot
of stony slopes. At the edge of the forest, many boulders, large and small,
litter the ground. Perhaps the result of landslides in the past, most of the
stones are long since covered by moss and lichen.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Many boulders, large and small, lie on the ground at the bottom of the slope to
the west, a sure sign of a rock slide in the past. Little but weed and dry
shrubs grow on the hard, stony ground, and there are not many signs of wildlife
in this region. Directly to the east, the hills give way to the malignant
forest.
name: Windswept Hills

desc:
This plateau is encircled by steep edges on three sides. To the south and east,
the dark roof of Mirkwood forest does not offer even a small glimpse of the
forest floor beneath and the dark canopy reaches almost up to the edges of the
plateau. To the west there is a long drop to the bottom of a ravine. Nothing
grows on the windswept rock and the only way off the plateau is into the hills
to the north.
name: On a Flat Shelf of Rock

desc:
A narrow, rocky trail lined by dry shrubs follows the downwards slope of a
ravine towards the south. Loose rocks clutter the trail and steady feet and good
balance are required to navigate it. Rocky protrusions extend out above the
ravine and sounds coming from atop these suggest that there might be bird's
nests up there.
name: Top of a Narrow Ravine

desc:
A narrow, rocky trail follows the descent of a ravine towards the west. Towards
the north, the end of the ravine can be seen against the sky. There is little
undergrowth here, for the ground is hard and dry, and lies in shadows much of
the day.
name: In a Narrow Ravine

desc:
A wide bed of flat rock, about twenty yards across, extends from the uneven rock
face to the west and across the floor of the ravine to the eastern side where it
disappears under a large overhang. The ravine continues out of sight towards the
north and south where the floor of the ravine itself is concealed by rocky
outcroppings and boulders, but the course of the ravine is visible as a jagged
scar through the hard stone.
name: At the Bottom of a Ravine

desc:
Large, cracked boulders lie scattered across the bottom of the ravine where they
have fallen from higher up in the hills. There is just enough space between
boulders to travel unhindered along the bottom of the ravine. The hard ground
and coarse sides of the ravine are mostly bare rock, although patches of dry
moss and small tufts of tough grass grow here and there.
name: At the Bottom of a Ravine

desc:
A low overhang extends out across the stony ground here, casting this part of
the ravine into dark shadow. There are many tracks on the hard ground, both
leading towards the south where the overhang continues, and into a dark cave
entrance to the east.
name: Beneath an Overhang

desc:
The steep, rugged rock faces crowd close on both sides, leaning inwards above
the narrow, rocky trail that makes a slow descent towards the north. Many
boulders, both large and small, lie along the trail, most of them long since
overgrown with patches of green moss. There are no signs of recent rock falls
although there are several deep cracks running up and down across the rock.
name: In a Narrow Ravine

desc:
A juncture has formed here. Rough, low openings lead into dark caves towards the
north and south, while a faint draft comes from the west, bringing fresh air
into the cave system. This room seems to serve as a watch post: several crude
wooden stools have been placed against the back wall and there are marks from
spear-butts in the dirt next to them, where guards lean their spears against the
wall.
name: Cavern Juncture

desc:
A large overhang extends above the hard, dusty soil here. A primitive stable has
been constructed against the back stone wall. There are many tracks of pawed
feet in the dust and scraps of raw, bloody meat lie strewn in one corner,
leftovers from a recent meal perhaps.
name: Beneath an Overhang

desc:
The rough, scabrous rock walls in this cavern are damp, almost moist. Large
sections of the stone are covered with growths of slimy, viscous brown mould.
Small stalactites hang down from the roof above like icicles, slowly dripping
percolated, calcareous water to the cavern floor.
name: In a Dank Tunnel

desc:
This cavern is in great disorder, the floor littered with all kinds of junk
related to warfare. Crates along the eastern wall are filled with crude armour,
much of it battered and broken. Several racks of weaponry stand leaning against
the north wall, the scimitars and axes in them notched and rusty. The top of a
crude wooden table in the middle of the room is the only space where there is
some order. A few leatherworker's tools lie on it, but otherwise it is free of
clutter.
name: A Crude Armoury

desc:
Filthy, dark water drips from the wet, uneven cavern ceiling above. Small
rivulets of dirty water trickle down along the walls to form shallow pools where
the walls meet the cavern floor. Wet, slimy mould grows across the walls,
filling the cave with a musty odour. The tunnel opens up towards the east, where
there seems to be a large cavern.
name: End of a Dark Tunnel

desc:
A wide, high cavity has formed beneath the hills here. The walls are of solid
rock and while jagged and uneven, there are no cracks or fissures and the risk
of a rock fall should be negligible. There are many signs of recent inhabitation
strewn about the large cave, and the inhabitants do not seem prone towards
keeping a neat, clean living space.
name: A Large Grotto

desc:
The coarse, jagged stone ceiling is very low in this natural cavern. Much of the
cave roof shows cracks and fissures, and every now and then dust and tiny rocks
sift down through the cracks and fall to the cavern floor. The cavern extends
towards the south, and the roof disappears higher into darkness in that
direction.
name: In a Natural Cavern

desc:
The stony ground is solid and free of loose rocks and undergrowth, providing a
wide, steady ledge cradled against the rock face. The ledge is, however,
slightly slanted outwards and a careless step might result in a tumble over the
edge. The ledge leads nowhere as it narrows toward both north and south, only to
completely disappear after less than twenty yards in either direction.
name: On a Wide Ledge

desc:
The ground here consists mostly of dry, hard earth. A few bushes and shrubs
struggle to survive on whatever moisture and sun that find their way down to the
bottom of the ravine. Several large, jagged boulders lie scattered across the
ground, partly buried in the soil and overgrown with moss.
name: Bottom of a Crevice

desc:
The sides of this crevice are rough and uneven, but the walls are far enough
apart that two horses could walk here side by side. The crevice leads down into
a ravine to the north, the descent steep but not unmanageably so. The ground is
solid rock for the most part, with patches of dirt held together by the roots of
tough mountain grass and small shrubs. To the south, the crevice ends in a black
thicket of tenacious-looking bushes.
name: Crevice in the Hills

desc:
The hills seem to form a natural hollow, fully occupied by tall, old trees.
Patches of rocks and standalone boulders are randomly strewn throughout the
area, partnering with dense shrubs and isolated groves of dark saplings. The
deep bowl formed by the hills makes passage in any direction other than north
impossible.
name: Hollow in the Hills

desc:
The stench of unwashed beings and excrement fills the air. The darkness spreads
greedily towards the entrance seeking to gobble up any natural light. Loose
shards of shale and grey stone shift underfoot and dust puffs up with each step.
Farther in, the darkness deepens to pitch black.
name: Dark, Oppressive Fissure

desc:
The ground here is covered in a thin layer of fog, making the surrounding forest
seem cold. The mist swirls with each step and keeps the surface of the othewise
dry ground damp. Several trees have fallen, apparently because of the loose soil
and their top-heavy branch load. Their rotting remains are festooned with white
and grey lichens and green mosses, which suck the remaining colour out of the
ancient brown trunks.
name: Murky Part of Mirkwood

desc:
The black trees grow tight and tall, creating small tunnels and narrow, twisting
passageways between the trunks and dense hanging branches. Darkness dominates
the area as thick foliage is not allowing daylight to shine upon the mossy
ground. Ominous-looking dark vines hang from the branches in abundance.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A wide, rough cavern opens up in the darkness. The ceiling is somewhere far
above, leaving enough space for a stone giant to stand in full height and still
be unable to reach anything. The walls are far from each other, there must be
tens of yards space between them. The ground is filled with gravel, rocks - and
half-eaten carcasses.
name: Shady, Rocky Cavern

desc:
Close to the cliff to the west and south the forest opens up somewhat;
nevertheless the trees atop the cliff fill eagerly all the space available,
blocking out the sky and never allowing sunlight to reach below the treetops
with more than a few indirect, weak rays. The cliff wall seems impossible to
scale, and to the north tall, dark and twisted trees grow densely making east
the only direction to travel forward.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A small cave opens up inside the rock. The stony walls are close to each other,
and the ceiling of black rock looms quite close above. The way out is located to
the east: a small hole which can be opened and closed with a loose boulder.
Small rocks and black gravel cover the floor.
name: Inside the Rock

desc:
Thick oozing vines hang from the branches, entangled to gnarled old trunks and
dense dark bushes growing all around the trees. Yet there are some open
passageways where trees do not grow, allowing travelling to all directions.
Above, partly inside the canopy, a dense network of grey spider silk can be
seen. The ground is filled with forest litter.
name: Deep within a Thick Forest

desc:
The dark, twisted trees grow in a tight semi-circle. Their low-hanging branches
and the wide spider webs that have been woven all around are resisting any
attempt to pass through, leaving south as the only possible way to travel
further in the ever dark and twisted Mirkwood. Dead branches and foliage cover
the ground in what appears to be a constant state of decay.
name: Dark, Twisted Trees

desc:
Despite the abundance of dead branches on the ground the trees seem to thrive
here, even though they are far darker and more twisted than any normal tree. The
forest is relatively open, allowing one to move on in any direction save to the
south where a large number of fallen trees, overgrown with vines and lichens,
prevents travel.
name: Dark, Twisted Trees

desc:
Around the dark trees masses of dark roots cover the ground, requiring care not
to trip over them or get stuck into some particularly twisted part. Black trees
are growing very densely to the north, preventing travelling in that direction.
A faint smell of rotting plants fills the air, though it is hard to make out
exactly what direction it comes from in this stale, windless place.
name: Twisting Roots

desc:
The dark, twisting, thick and sinister forest grows densely here. Giant bushes
cover the ground, with the occasional tree-root visible between them; tangled
vines hang from the boughs. The forest continues to all directions, the only
exception being to the east where one can make out the shape of a rising cliff,
but only vaguely.
name: Deep within a Thick Forest

desc:
With their roots firmly entrenched in the ground, the high-reaching trees have
twisted their branches and crowns around each other, making it impossible to
tell where one tree ends and another begins. A wide, darkling crevice to the
south makes it impossible to travel in that direction. To the north a wall of
trees rises, seemingly impenetrable.
name: Ominous-looking Crevice under the Dark, Twisted Trees

desc:
The ground in this part of the forest is covered with thorny bushes and tangled
shrubs. Long, slithering vines with short, hook-like spikes crawl through and
across the dense undergrowth, creating an ever worse tangle. Amidst the
abundance of undergrowth, dark boles rise up, wide boughs spreading out and
forming a thick foliage above that casts the forest into deep shadows.
name: Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
Great tangles of brush and vines block the way towards the south and west.
Towards the east and north, the undergrowth is also very dense but not
impassably so, although getting stung, snagged and scratched by the many sharp
thorns is unavoidable. The forest floor is shrouded in dark shadows by the dense
foliage above that hides the ground from the skies.
name: Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
Gigantic, gnarled trees stand next to each other, rising to great heights.
Somewhere far above in the dark, twisted branches, vines and huge, black leaves
create an unbroken canopy over the forest. At ground level, great tangles of
thorny brush and large, green ferns make passage very difficult, blocking the
way altogether towards the east and west, while a muddy slope is rising steeply
upwards towards the north.
name: At the Bottom of a Muddy Slope

desc:
Large, tangled brambles covered in sharp thorns spread out across the entire
valley floor. The valley goes up a steep slope to the south which seems to be
the only way to leave it. In the deepest part of the valley, towards the west,
the ground is wet and bog-like, and a filthy-looking pool has formed there.
name: In a Thorny Valley

desc:
A huge oak has fallen westwards. Its massive, hollow trunk forms a tunnel, which
crosses the deep crevice to the west. Even though the crevice is too wide for
anyone to jump over it, still the trees grow high and wide enough to block out
the sky above it. Densely growing trees to the south create an effective barrier
against travel. To the north the dense forest shows an opening that would be
possible to get through.
name: By a Huge, Fallen Oak Trunk

desc:
Low shrubs and masses of dead leaves cover the ground and hinder movement. They
make a faint rustling sound as one moves through them. The thick mass of trees
opens up to the north and west, while a mix of large beech trees and a dense
thorn hedge makes passage impossible to the south. To the east a cliff cuts off
the way.
name: Deep within a Thick Forest

desc:
The ground rises slightly to form a small hill covered with the same dark and
twisted trees that are surrounding it. On the north side a number of trees have
died some time ago, and now their vine- and lichen-covered trunks create a
barrier that blocks travel in that direction. To the south dark, thick and
leathery vines make up a wall. To the west the trees grow quite densely but it
could be possible to pass through the mass of entwined branches.
name: Small Hill in a Twisted Forest

desc:
Sinister Mirkwood continues in all directions but to the east, where a cliff
rises preventing any passage beyond it. The trees of this area are rising to
great heights, their thick trunks disappearing from the sight to the shadows of
the canopy above. Bleached skeletons lie on the ground, half-concealed under the
weeds, brush and other litter.
name: Deep within a Thick Forest

desc:
Black, twisted trees grow high and so close to each other that their trunks and
numerous boughs form small tunnels between the trees. This makes visibility very
limited and navigation rather confusing. A thick wall to the west would seems to
prevent passage to that direction. To the south it seems to be possible to push
between the trunks and through some low-hanging branches, though there is no way
telling what awaits on the other side. A faint smell of decay permeates the air.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A giant spider web spreads through the forest, reaching from tree to tree, from
branch to branch and from the dark canopy to the ground. The web is sticky-
looking, and at some parts even several inches thick. Some carcasses are hanging
in the air, birds, squirrels and a humanoid wrapped in grey cocoon. The trees of
the area look grey and putrid. The undergrowth consists of branches intertwined
with the thorny brush, slowing the advancement of anyone journeying here.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Deep inside the tunnels, the air is noticeably cooler and the smell of offal has
grown stronger. As one goes deeper, the roof becomes higher and the floor slopes
very deeply down into the depths beyond. Faint grunting noises echo all around
as well as sounds of crunching and snarling, that seem to mainly come from the
east.
name: In a Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel continues for maybe thirty yards, roughly in a north-south direction.
There is some kind of junction to the north and to the south the way ends
abruptly in a solid wall of stone. The walls, floor and ceiling are dark, cold
stone.
name: Dead-end

desc:
Out of the other caves' darkness springs forth an unusual sight: a large cavern
full of crystalline formations and projections. A dim green-tinted glow can be
detected deep inside the floor and walls giving this space faint illumination.
Crystal stalactites and stalagmites jut from the ceiling and floor while quartz
shards litter the ground.
name: A Crystal Cave

desc:
The tunnel circles around a large crystal boulder which is more than two yards
wide and at least three feet tall. Its surface is perfectly smooth as if melted
by dragon's fire or some other immense heat. The boulder is glowing faintly,
lighting the room with reflections of red, purple and green. A few giant
stalactites are hanging from the ceiling, while sharp stones which look like
gems stick out from the floor.
name: Eerie Reflections of Light

desc:
Jet-black gravel and occasional bones cover the floor of this dark tunnel. To
the west there is a hole in the ceiling revealing a steep tube from above, and
next to it is another passage which slopes gently upwards and northwards where
the tunnel seems to expand into a more open cavern. A confusing network of
smaller passages lead towards the north-east and east. There is a black hole in
the tunnel floor, leading downwards into the darkness.
name: Deep in the Caves

desc:
A narrow tunnel cuts through the cold soil. The walls are partly blackstone
mixed with gravel, clay and dirt. Huge roots snake in and out, helping to hold
the soil in place. Old bones and shattered rocks line the floor. To the south,
the earth has collapsed in a rather abrupt manner, denying any further passage.
name: Dead-end

desc:
A confusing network of tunnels runs through the stone. They twist to and fro,
making it hard to see very far ahead. The walls reach in towards each other as
they rise, so that the ceilings of these tunnels form natural arches, spoiled
only by an occasional ledge high above the ground. Some foot prints are visible
on the floor: someone or something has been wandering here not so long ago.
name: Snaking Tunnels

desc:
A wide tunnel runs through the stone, making a long bend from the west to the
south and then meeting a sturdy door which blocks the passageway. Small rocks
and dust litter the floor, giving an impression that this tunnel is not in
frequent use, although large footprints that lead through the area indicate that
someone, or something, has been here not so long ago. Occasional ledges and
small cracks create deep shadows on the rocky walls.
name: Tunnel inside the Earth

desc:
Littered with bones, broken armour, scraps of fabric, and other castoffs, this
area is obviously used by trolls. In one corner, a pile of pine needles has been
thrown in an attempt to make a bed. The remains of former meals are strewn
across the dirty, hard-packed floor. A sturdy, crude door is located the the
east.
name: A Filthy Chamber

desc:
The passageway transforms into a long, twisting slope rising towards the west
and descending to the east. There is a number of high, crude stepping stones on
the path, every single one of them being more than two feet tall, and several
yards long. At the top of the slope there is a crude but sturdy-looking door to
the north. The visibility is rather limited due to angular steps and numerous
bends on the path. The ceiling of the tunnel looms somewhere above, mostly
hidden in the dark.
name: Long, Crude Steps

desc:
Large, jagged boulders and smaller rocks border this snaking passageway which
runs in an west-east direction through the rock. The way is rather wide and
rises upward to the west. On both sides of the passage, the walls rise steeply
upward, like in a steep ravine. The rocky ceiling is far above, with some grey
stalactites hanging from it.
name: Underground Ravine

desc:
This round, rocky cavern is located inside the stone. Its floor is approximately
ten feet wide, the walls are formed of grey stone, while the ceiling looms
somewhere above in the dark. Some smooth rocks have been placed in a circle
giving the impression that they have been used as benches. There is an exit out
to the east, with a large boulder used as the door. A crude bar mechanism has
been bolted to the boulder making it possible to operate it. At the opposite end
of the cavern a passageway leads deeper inside the hill.
name: Hidden Gate

desc:
The prison cell is a damp and dark room. It has been dug into the dark rock,
offering no exit out save to west where is a sturdy-looking stone door. There
are rusty shackles on the walls, some of them still containing remains of
unlucky prisoners. The floor is littered with bones, some of them apparently
gnawed with large, strong jaws.
name: Bone-littered Prison Cell

desc:
The thick, evergreen undergrowth forms darker, tangled shapes in the deep gloom.
Trees rise like twisted, oddly formed pillars from among the dense undergrowth.
The shadows cast by the gnarled trunks, a deeper black in a world of shadows,
seem to quiver and shake with barely restrained malice. Shadows of thick
branches reach out across the ground, shadows that do not always correspond to
any real branches above. Across it all layers of sticky, grey spider webs have
been spun. In some webs the small dark shapes of Mirkwood moths and butterflies
can be seen.
name: Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
Close to the edge of the forest, a couple of small hills rise towards the sky.
Healthy green in colour, the grass of these hills thrives as the gloomy shadow
of the forest does not reach here. Some low bushes and small patches of ferns
stand out in the vegetation.
name: Verdant Hills

desc:
The high, dense trees prevent effectively with their thick bole and concealing
crowns any sunlight to reach the ground, making any form of undergrowth life
impossible. Moist soil covers the ground, fallen branches and twigs are lying
here, slowly disintegrating.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
Completely surrounded by high beeches and oaks, one might lose all perspective
and orientation here. The rough ground with its thorny bushes makes it difficult
to traverse the area. A lot of tree roots stick up from the ground, further
hindering advancement into the forest.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
With white and green lichens growing on the towering, ancient trees and green,
fluffy moss covering the ground, this forest seems to have been left undisturbed
and preserved for a very long time. Vines are climbing the trees, not minding
the years rolling by as they reach for the treetops.
name: Untouched Forest

desc:
Bordering the great forest, these hills bear surprisingly little vegetation in
the form of trees. The ground is covered with small patches of grass and a few
bushes. Other than that, the land is rocky, covered with dust and the occasional
monolithic boulder.
name: Sparsely-vegetated Hills

desc:
The trees here grow extremely close and impressively high, blocking even a
single sunbeam from reaching the ground with their crowns and thick trunks. A
few rotten tree trunks lie scattered around here, slowly decomposing and
emitting a foul stench.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Tall, thick oak and birch stretch ever skyward, outdoing each other to gobble
any available light to ensure their growth. At ground level, a deep gloom
settles like a thick blanket, making vision difficult at best. Small rodents and
forest creatures scurry into hiding with each noise.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Under the intact canopy of the ancient, malicious trees, the ground is covered
with spiny bushes and thorny shrubs, making sure any traveller will have a tough
time passing through. Ominous roots, covered with dead leaves, penetrate the
soil, further complicating advancement. Northwards, a rise in height can be
noticed.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Dense groves and tightly-packed stands of oak of Mirkwood are blocking out the
light and hindering the passage. Debris from the trees litter the ground,
decomposing in putrefaction. Several trees have collapsed, fungus covering their
broken, rotting, corpse-like trunks.
name: Dense Forest on a Slope

desc:
Mirkwood forest has spread up these hills, and the trees shade the ground
heavily, making it difficult for the undergrowth to flourish. The soil is moist,
but drinkable water can not be found. Moss can be found growing on several
trees, as well as on some boulders.
name: Wooded Slope

desc:
The dark beeches and blasted oaks twist together in a visually confusing mesh,
deceiving the senses. Layered with tangles of vines and weeds, the forest seems
to be menacing any living creature that is not some form of plant. Thick tree
crowns overhead block direct light, making navigation near impossible.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
This cliff rises high over the Old Forest Road, thereby providing one with an
exceptionally good view. Except for the good view, the cliff has nothing to
offer, being bare and devoid of any vegetation. The cliff ends abruptly to the
south: there is a steep fall down to the road below.
name: Cliff Overlooking the Old Forest Road

desc:
These hills are totally abandoned by all kinds of plant life. The terrain is
very uneven making the travelling hard; steep hillsides are filled with sharp
rocks and the edges of occasional gorges look unstable, as if collapsing at any
moment. All around, dust and detritus cover the surface of the ground.
name: Forsaken Hills

desc:
Trees grow tall in this uneven landscape, preventing one from taking advantage
of the high ground in order to scout the surrounding area. Lichens have formed
on several of the trees, bringing some variation to the landscape. The ground is
covered with ferns and low, thorny bushes.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
The great forest has spread to the top of this hill, despite the high altitude.
The trees here, however, seem to have some trouble to flourish and grow more
sparsely than in the adjacent parts of the forest. A steep hillside to the east
and unfriendly vegetation to the north and south leaves only one way to get away
from the hilltop.
name: Forest-covered Hilltop

desc:
Huge, onyx-black trees stand at the border of the forest, leaving just enough
room between the trunks to allow passage to and from the forest. A row of gloomy
hills, sloping steeply up at the edge of the forest, are seen all around save in
the west.
name: Forest below the Hills

desc:
An ancient forest road can be vaguely observed to the south, made almost
completely invisible by the thick forest. The undergrowth seems somewhat
trampled and some trees bear marks from knives, indicating that humanoids have
left the road at this place.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The ground slopes heavily here, getting higher to the east. Trees grow sparsely
here, though still not sparse enough to provide the undergrowth with sufficient
sunlight. Several tree species grow here, beeches and oaks being the most
commonly occuring.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The steep ravine's walls are full of needle-sharp rocks. There are also big
boulders, small stones, medium-sized rocks and the common feature for them is
that they are all rock-hard and sharp. Some dry grass tufts and occasional
bleached bones have stuck to the wall. When your body lands on the hard ravine
wall still high above the ground, you barely have time to think that this was
it...
name: Ravine Walls

desc:
A deep, bottomless-looking crevice opens up in the ground. It almost surrounds
this brush-filled ledge, leaving level ground only to the south. On the opposite
side of the crevice the trees are growing tall, dark and extremely twisted; it
looks almost as if they are trying to strangle each other. To the east, the land
on the other side of the crevice descends lower and a huge stock reaches out
across the chasm. The trunk is hollow, and it looks possible that one could walk
inside it, across the abyss to the other side, although the trunk does not seem
to be very firmly attached to where it lies now.
name: Sloping Ground before the Abyss

desc:
You slide downwards on the rocky and almost vertical wall of this dark, damp
shaft. The walls do not offer a lot of support for safe climbing, although you
see a few branches and some roots pass by, out of reach, too far to help in any
attempt to climb back to safety. Down below, the rock-filled bottom of the
crevice gets closer: being full of rocks, it does not promise a soft landing!
name: Rocky Walls of the Bottomless Crevice

desc:
You slide downwards on the rocky and almost vertical wall of this dark, damp
shaft. The walls do not offer a lot of support for safe climbing, although you
see a few branches and some roots pass by, out of reach, too far to help in any
attempt to climb back to safety. Down below, the rock-filled bottom of the
crevice gets closer: being full of rocks, it does not promise a soft landing!
name: Rocky Walls of the Bottomless Crevice

desc:
Inside the dark, fallen trunk the air is moist, and the surface of the
hollowed-out tree seems wet to the touch. There are numerous track-like markings
all over the walls, clear and visible paths of different bugs who once thrived
here. The floor has been worn down from thousands of steps over the years and is
very smooth to walk upon. A torn-down bird's nest and pieces of eggshells lie on
the ground.
name: An Oaken Trunk

desc:
The hollow oaken trunk is rather tight here, but seems to expand towards the
west. The round walls bear countless scratch marks, as though rough metal armour
and various weapons have time and again rubbed against the wood. As well as
those, there are the distinctive marks of digging bugs. The bumpy inside of the
tree makes a good solid roof, and the closed, narrow space makes every little
sound echo several times. A small pool of mud is collecting in a cavity on the
floor.
name: A Narrow Trunk

desc:
Thousands of thick boughs covered in a multitude of black leaves stretch out
above, shrouding the forest in an unbroken gloom. Under this oppressive, intact
canopy the ground itself is soft, moist and covered with surprisingly abundant
plant life. The earth falls away towards the south and there is a steep, muddy
slope there.
name: At the Top of a Muddy Slope

desc:
Much of the undergrowth in this area grows in perpetual gloom, yet even so dark
silhouettes of thorny bushes can be seen in every direction. The boles of the
old, gnarled trees standing here are overgrown with large, ensnaring vines and
clusters of dark fungi. The ground is descending heavily downwards to the south,
and transforms into a wet and marsh-like, shallow valley.
name: Entangled, Dark Forest

desc:
You slide downwards on the rocky and almost vertical wall of this dark, damp
shaft. The walls do not offer a lot of support for safe climbing, although you
see a few branches and some roots pass by, out of reach, too far to help in any
attempt to climb back to safety. Down below, the rock-filled bottom of the
crevice gets closer: being full of rocks, it does not promise a soft landing!
name: Rocky Walls of the Bottomless Crevice

desc:
The valley is shallow, barely more than a depression in the forest floor. Huge,
tangled bushes fill the entire floor of the valley, limiting visibility and the
possibility for travel greatly. The ground is wet and soft and covered by wet,
black moss wherever there is a reprieve from the dense undergrowth. At the very
bottom of the hollow lies a murky, dirty-looking pond.
name: In a Thorny Valley

desc:
Dark gloom shrouds the forest in this dismal corner of Mirkwood. Strange, dark
trees possessing forms and shapes no tree should rightfully have loom in the
shadows like malevolent beings with evil intent. Strange, dark choking vines
snake and twist their way up the queer trunks and thick, knotted roots, oddly
pale and flesh-like, poke up through the ground.
name: At the Fringe of a Corrupted Forest

desc:
The canopy of the dark, malignant forest hangs low. Its black leaves,
uncountable twigs and occasional vines create an intact roof over the forest's
floor, completely preventing sunlight from reaching this place. Behind every
single gnarled trunk there are more trunks to be seen, and more bushes to get
scratched from, and more roots to get entangled in.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Mossy boulders stand so tightly side by side that the way through this area is
just a narrow, leaf-covered path between the rocks. Dark oaks stand silently,
their crowns disappearing into the darkness above and their roots entangling the
rocks. Grey- and blue-coloured spider webs have been woven between the trees.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Long, twisted and heavy branches seem to grasp all around for anything to hold,
to crush, to suffocate, to kill; to take from the others what the trees do not
have but yearn for, to ease their endless torment. In some places bones can be
seen half-sunken into the trees: some small, some large, all victims of the
trees' malice and hatred against all other living things. Deep, long grooves
cover the ground as if something huge and heavy has been moving around here.
name: Centre of the Twisted Woods

desc:
The surrounding woods seem wilder and even more twisted than other parts of the
forest, giving an impression that something is infusing them with power and
purpose, so that the trees are now trying to escape their age-long imprisonment
to the earth. Gnarled roots have found their way above the ground, trunks are
leaning to the north, and huge, thick boughs are twisting to each other, as if
trying to grab support for movement. A distinct and clear smell of rotting
plants comes from somewhere close.
name: Twisted, Dark Woods

desc:
The huge, dark, and heavy trees that grow in this oppressive corner of Mirkwood
rise higher than most of the other trees, as if being nurtured by something the
other trees are denied. The sheer size of the trees opens some space, but to the
north and east the forest quickly thickens again and thus prevents passage. The
trees look almost as if they were trying to lift up their roots from the ground
and walk. A distinct smell of decay wafts in the air.
name: Twisted, Dark Woods

desc:
This part of the forest is warmer and more humid than the rest, leading to much
quicker and more noticeable rotting of dead plants, leaves, and branches. The
stench of decay hangs heavy in the air. While the decay is clearly visible on
the ground, the rest of the forest seems strong and full of life, though dark,
and twisted. To the south and east thick walls of densely packed trees allow
passage only to insects and other small animals.
name: Rotting Plants

desc:
The twisted, gnarled tree trunks standing here are overgrown with clusters of
pallid, corpse-grey fungi and thick layers of mould. A heavy stench of decay and
death enshrouds the trees and undergrowth like a fog. Tangles of thorny bushes
and sticky-looking spider webs make the area hard to pass through and great care
is required to not get snagged or caught on needle-sharp spines.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Impassable walls of entangled bramble and brush surround this area to the west,
east and north. Towards the south passage seems possible, yet even in that
direction one might get snagged and prickled by thorny bushes, or get stuck in
the thick, smelly spider webs that have been stretched between the trunks. Oak,
beech and fir grow among the tangle, the treetops forming a dark, green ceiling
above that casts the forest into deep shadow.
name: In a Dark Corner of Mirkwood

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Dense thorn bushes and bristling trees crowd in from all sides, making travel in
most directions basically impossible. Thick moss dangles just above and vines
seem to catch at the feet from below. A dim illumination permeates the area,
although the forest reduces visibility and hinders any sense of distance.
name: Dead End Forest

desc:
Without warning a high cliff rises from the ground to the west and north. Tall,
dark-green shrubs cover most of the base of the cliff, giving way to huge trees
that stand in silence a few yards away from the cliff. One can proceed from here
to the south and east, far below the dark canopy which is blocking out the sky,
allowing no light for the creatures and plants below.
name: Corner in the Dark Woods

desc:
The filthy, brown pool is full of dead, rotting plants floating weightlessly,
suspended in the still water. Vague, slithering silhouettes can be seen in the
murky darkness, yet whether they are dead, floating vines and branches, or
something more sinister, is hard to discern.
name: In a Stinking, Murky Pool

desc:
The water is murky and full of floating parts of branches, dead plants and
leaves. The bottom of this pond is muddy and engulfing, and one has to be
careful to not get stuck there. Among the slimy algae, slithering shapes seem to
move in the darkness, although it might be dead vines floating in the brown
water.
name: In a Murky Pool

desc:
Massive expanses of thorny bramble completely fill the spaces between hoary
trunks of oak and beech. Thick, corpse-grey strands of lichen cover the
brambles, resembling large cobwebs lying atop the brush in tangled heaps. A few
open passageways can be discerned in the gloom where patches of black moss are
all that grow on the soft ground.
name: Deep in the Tangled Forest

desc:
A veritable riot of tangled bushes, knotted roots, low-hanging spider webs and
scattered boulders make these woods a nightmare to traverse. The deep shadows
further add to the sense of quiet foreboding that emanates from the immemorial
forest. Patches of black moss grow on boulders and tree-trunks alike, filling
the air with a faint scent of decay.
name: Low-hanging Spider Webs

desc:
A mixed forest of oak, beech and fir grows here. The timeworn trunks have been
overgrown with grey lichen and moss, and every single branch of the trees seems
to be connected to each other by a confusing network of cobwebs. Dense thickets
of thorny undergrowth further add to the gloomy, eerie atmosphere. Creaking,
scratching sounds come from the undergrowth as entangled branches brush against
each other.
name: Hoary Trees Covered in Spider Webs

desc:
Large, tangled bushes, full of thorny branches that could snag or prick anyone
trying to pass through them, fill the spaces between the ancient, gnarled trunks
of the Mirkwood forest. The gloom here on the forest floor is perpetual, the
dense canopy above preventing any light from reaching the ground. There is a
constant creaking, rustling sound of branches and bushes shifting and settling
in new positions.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
This entire area is overgrown with huge, tangled bushes. The dark trunks of
ancient trees rise among the dense undergrowth and large, knotted roots poke
through the layer of leaves rotting away on the ground. There is a constant
creaking and scraping sound as branches and bushes brush against each other,
settling into new positions.
name: Deep in the Tangled Forest

desc:
In between the ancient, twisted trunks of oak and beech, a mass of tangled,
intertwining undergrowth creates a formidable maze to travel through. Grey
strands of lichen hang down from thick, heavily-leaved boughs and the forest
lies in a perpetual gloom. The air smells of decomposing vegetation and the
forest floor is covered in layers of rotting leaves.
name: Deep in a Tangled Forest

desc:
Brown, murky mud floats all around, making it difficult to tell where the water
of this pond actually ends and the bottom starts. The dark water is dense, full
of dirty sediment, rotten algae and other particles. The muddy bottom is
engulfing, making it hard to move around - or to the surface!
name: Buried within Mud

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
This part of Mirkwood has a sullen feel to it. The trees seem to be angry at the
disturbance yet too depressed to care much. Thick bushes and thin saplings fill
in the spaces between the large trunks while decaying leaves mix with needles
all over the ground.
name: Glum Forest

desc:
The gnarled boles of ancient trees loom like troubled shades in the gloom. Vast
spider webs stretch between branches and trunks, far too large to be meant to
catch butterflies or moths. The ground is mostly bare of undergrowth, covered
only in dead leaves, fir needles and patches of dark, moist moss.
name: Deep in a Web-entangled Forest

desc:
The gnarled boles of ancient trees loom like troubled shades in the gloom. Vast
spider webs stretch between branches and trunks, far too large to be meant to
catch butterflies or moths. The ground is mostly bare of undergrowth, covered
only in dead leaves, fir needles and patches of dark, moist moss.
name: Deep in a Web-entangled Forest

desc:
The gnarled boles of ancient trees loom like troubled shades in the gloom. Vast
spider webs stretch between branches and trunks, far too large to be meant to
catch butterflies or moths. The ground is mostly bare of undergrowth, covered
only in dead leaves, fir needles and patches of dark, moist moss.
name: Deep in a Web-entangled Forest

desc:
The gnarled boles of ancient trees loom like troubled shades in the gloom. Vast
spider webs stretch between branches and trunks, far too large to be meant to
catch butterflies or moths. The ground is mostly bare of undergrowth, covered
only in dead leaves, fir needles and patches of dark, moist moss.
name: Deep in a Web-entangled Forest

desc:
The thick, evergreen undergrowth forms darker, tangled shapes in the deep gloom.
Trees rise like twisted, oddly formed pillars from among the dense undergrowth.
The shadows cast by the gnarled trunks, a deeper black in a world of shadows,
seem to quiver and shake with barely restrained malice. Shadows of thick
branches reach out across the ground, shadows that do not always correspond to
any real branches above. Across it all layers of sticky, grey spider webs have
been spun. In some webs the small dark shapes of Mirkwood moths and butterflies
can be seen.
name: Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
The thick, evergreen undergrowth forms darker, tangled shapes in the deep gloom.
Trees rise like twisted, oddly formed pillars from among the dense undergrowth.
The shadows cast by the gnarled trunks, a deeper black in a world of shadows,
seem to quiver and shake with barely restrained malice. Shadows of thick
branches reach out across the ground, shadows that do not always correspond to
any real branches above. Across it all layers of sticky, grey spider webs have
been spun. In some webs the small dark shapes of Mirkwood moths and butterflies
can be seen.
name: Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
The thick, evergreen undergrowth forms darker, tangled shapes in the deep gloom.
Trees rise like twisted, oddly formed pillars from among the dense undergrowth.
The shadows cast by the gnarled trunks, a deeper black in a world of shadows,
seem to quiver and shake with barely restrained malice. Shadows of thick
branches reach out across the ground, shadows that do not always correspond to
any real branches above. Across it all layers of sticky, grey spider webs have
been spun. In some webs the small dark shapes of Mirkwood moths and butterflies
can be seen.
name: Tangled Undergrowth
